html:
Exclusive to:

Neon Quattro are a Fish Food Item introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. They can be obtained by stocking Fish Farms with Roe, together with Purple Groupers, Pierrot Fish and Tropical Fish. They can be used in the Crock Pot, and count as 0.5 units of Meat and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the Tropical Bouillabaisse. Neon Quattro cannot be dried using a Drying Rack.

When consumed, they will immediately lower the player's temperature by 8 degrees.

Cooked Neon Quattro

A Cooked Neon Quattro is a Neon Quattro that has been cooked over a Campfire, Fire Pit, Chiminea or dwarf star It can also be used in various Crock Pot recipes. As with the uncooked variant, Cooked Neon Quattro will lower the player's temperature by 3 degrees when consumed.

Usage

Trivia

  • Neon Quattro Fish were added to the Shipwrecked DLC more than a year after its official completion in the Home Sea Home update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
  • According to Wickerbottom, the Neon Quattro is a close relative to the neon tetra, a species of freshwater fish in real life. However, the neon tetra is much smaller than the Neon Quattro appears in the game.
    • The name "Quattro" is most likely a reference to this, with the word tetra meaning "four" in Greek, and quattro meaning "four" in Italian.

Gallery

  • Wigfrid standing next to a Neon Quattro and a Cooked Neon Quattro on the ground.
  • A Fish Farm stocking Neon Quattro Fish, visible from the cyan fish on the small sign.
  • Neon Quattro as seen in the poster for the Home Sea Home update.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“It looks like a fish, but it feels clammy.”
–Wilson, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Is living underwater as awful as it sounds?”
–Willow, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Ha ha! Scales is chilly!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“It misses its watery home.”
–Wendy, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“THIS FISH IS DRIER THAN THE OTHERS”
–WX-78, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Closely related to Paracheirodon innesi.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Nice colours on that one.”
–Woodie, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Don't you ever blink?”
–Maxwell, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Quite an exotic selection of fish here. All inedible raw.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Thöu shalt swim nö möre.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“You're so slimy!”
–Webber, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“What a cute lil dude!”
–Walani, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Would you prefer to be scorched, or cracked?”
–Warly, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Inta th'pot w'ya.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“'TIS A BLUE FISHY”
–Wilba, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“Big Lip Glub Glub”
–Wormwood, when examining a Neon Quattro.
“It's a fish. I'll bet you a nickel I can find another one.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Neon Quattro.
Neon Quattro are a Fish Food Item introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. They can be obtained by stocking Fish Farms with Roe, together with Purple Groupers, Pierrot Fish and Tropical Fish. They can be used in the Crock Pot, and count as 0.5 units of Meat and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the Tropical Bouillabaisse. Neon Quattro cannot be dried using a Drying Rack.
When consumed, they will immediately lower the player's temperature by 8 degrees.

“Fried fry.”
–Wilson, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“The fire got rid of most of the fish smell.”
–Willow, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Toasty fish is good muscle fuel.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“I've... eaten worse...”
–Wendy, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“I WILL GAIN ITS DRYING POWERS”
–WX-78, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“A cousin of Paracheirodon innesi, ready for dinner.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Looks like I'll be eating for a day.”
–Woodie, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Don't worry, there's plenty of them in the sea.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“A pleasant enough presentation.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Njörd gives me my fill this day.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Mmm, smells good!”
–Webber, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Sorry. I still gotta eat.”
–Walani, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Sigh. It's not even seasoned.”
–Warly, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Th'bread'n'butter'o'th'sea!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“WILBA WILL'ST EAT-ETH”
–Wilba, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“Fins are yummy”
–Wormwood, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
“At least the tail is off.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Cooked Neon Quattro.
A Cooked Neon Quattro is a Neon Quattro that has been cooked over a Campfire, Fire Pit, Chiminea or dwarf star It can also be used in various Crock Pot recipes. As with the uncooked variant, Cooked Neon Quattro will lower the player's temperature by 3 degrees when consumed.
Usage[edit | edit source]
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Neon Quattro Fish were added to the Shipwrecked DLC more than a year after its official completion in the Home Sea Home update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
According to Wickerbottom, the Neon Quattro is a close relative to the neon tetra, a species of freshwater fish in real life. However, the neon tetra is much smaller than the Neon Quattro appears in the game.
The name "Quattro" is most likely a reference to this, with the word tetra meaning "four" in Greek, and quattro meaning "four" in Italian.

'''Neon Quattro''' are a [[Fishes|Fish]] [[Food]] [[Item]] introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. They can be obtained by stocking [[Fish Farm]]s with [[Roe]], together with [[Purple Grouper]]s, [[Pierrot Fish]] and [[Tropical Fish]]. They can be used in the [[Crock Pot]], and count as 0.5 units of [[Meat]] and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the [[Tropical Bouillabaisse]]. Neon Quattro cannot be dried using a [[Drying Rack]].

When consumed, they will immediately lower the player's temperature by 8 degrees.

=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* Neon Quattro Fish were added to the ''Shipwrecked'' DLC more than a year after its official completion in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked#November 21, 2017 - Home Sea Home Update|Home Sea Home]]'' update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
* According to Wickerbottom, the Neon Quattro is a close relative to the [[wikipedia:Neon_tetra|neon tetra]], a species of freshwater fish in real life. However, the neon tetra is much smaller than the Neon Quattro appears in the game.
** The name "Quattro" is most likely a reference to this, with the word ''tetra'' meaning "four" in Greek, and ''quattro'' meaning "four" in Italian.
